what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block that argues for a UI-mode commenting strategy: anchor comments to a stable semantic `ui://` identity (via `data-ui-ref`) instead of screen coordinates, and fold the resolved `code://` location into the comment envelope at the time the comment is created.
observations: Title is 'P8 · 7. UI mode = Figma's nested-anchoring problem, and you have the cure Fig'
Identifies Figma's limitation that comments do not follow nested elements because they anchor to coordinates/top-frame
Proposes `data-ui-ref` → `ui://` as a stable semantic identity independent of position
Recommends anchoring UI-mode comments to `ui://` identity and re-finding the element after re-render
Describes `ui:// → code:// → file + line + dependencies` as deep-linking a comment to editable source
Says the resolved code location should be captured in the envelope at mint time, not read time
Mentions re-verifying the resolved location against current source on open, using the same stale-detection as §1
standouts: Anchor UI-mode comments to the `ui://` identity, never to screen coordinates
Fold the resolved `code://` location into the envelope at mint time
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, history
Uses a `board://` URI as the item address
Includes a `part_of` link to another board item
concerns: Makes a strong competitive claim ('beat the famous tool', 'cure Figma lacks') without providing implementation evidence in this file
Title appears truncated ('Fig' at the end)
notes: A design-principle note insisting that UI comments bind to stable element identities and carry mint-time source links so they survive layout changes.
questions: What is the exact URI scheme and assignment mechanism for `ui://` and `code://`?
How is `data-ui-ref` generated and guaranteed to be stable across renders?
What does 'envelope at mint time' mean structurally?
How is stale detection implemented, and what is described in §1?
What is 'P8' and what is the parent item `board://item-64466290`?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to prescribe a UI-mode anchoring and code-linking strategy, and the content does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Stable semantic identity over positional coordinates
Deep-linking comments to editable source code
Stale detection and re-verification on read
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a hard design requirement for a UI review system: comments must remain attached to the correct UI element even when layouts re-render, and must point directly to the source code that produced that element.
novelty: The distinct proposal is using a `ui://` semantic identity to escape coordinate-based anchoring and mint-time `code://` resolution to tie a UI comment to its editable source at the moment it is created.
contribution: This file contributes the principle that UI comments should anchor to stable `ui://` references and carry a mint-time `code://` resolution, supporting layout-resilient comments and source-linked review workflows.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item prescribing that UI-mode comments anchor to stable `ui://` identities rather than screen coordinates and capture resolved `code://` source locations at creation time.
intention_for_embedding: To define a UI commenting anchoring rule that prevents coordinate-based drift and links comments directly to source code at the moment they are created.
